text: I trusted you. I did not love you. Love is for mothers and fools. I trusted you.
chapterTitle: Chapter II: The Price of Trust

text: The Iron Bank leaves records where men leave lies.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: Did I not tell you to stop questioning things?
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Flowers and bugs cannot stand against dragons.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: You truly were a loyal Hand... until you weren't.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: The King does not serve. The King grants. You serve.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Stags are merely meals for dragons.
chapterTitle: Chapter V: Fathers and Their Sins

text: Tell me, Maester Cordin, will there ever be a moment at this table where you offer a thought of your own?
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: I just need to sleep.
chapterTitle: Chapter II: The Weight of Loyalty

text: A Baratheon kneels when he feels safe.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: You know what the problem with the Kingsguard is? They pass over fixers—real men like me—and hire prancing pretty boys who spend their mornings brushing their hair.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Make one more out-of-place joke, Saathos, and I'll cut off your little Saathos and hand it to you.
chapterTitle: Chapter V: Fathers and Their Sins

text: Your body is here. The rest of you left this hall hours ago.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: My king, will you punish years of loyal service for the deeds of one stranger?
chapterTitle: Chapter I: The Poison Beneath the Crown

text: Next time, remember who it is you dare disappoint.
chapterTitle: Chapter II: The Price of Trust

text: Love alone cannot preserve a dynasty.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: Sons must pay the price for their fathers' ambitions.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Anyway, trust is more important than love.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Gaelor and Baelor, eh?
chapterTitle: Chapter V: Fathers and Their Sins

text: No one turns their back on me and my family.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: Knights of Oldtown! Your Lord was murdered under a banner of peace! Kill Gaelor! Kill them all!
chapterTitle: Chapter V: Fathers and Their Sins

text: I have never seen a man as spineless as you.
chapterTitle: Chapter II: The Price of Trust

text: I spent years believing I could solve everything if I simply thought long enough. I was wrong.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: You will become heir to the Iron Throne. I did not ask whether you wanted it.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: The people have suffered enough. They will not pay for a royal celebration.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: I've fought men twice my size. They frightened me less.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty
note: to Naella, on their wedding day

text: You should be king. Not Gaelor. You.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: If that is the King’s will.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: The only reason I am alive is King Baelenys.
chapterTitle: Chapter III: The Weight of Loyalty

text: Jace has no desire to rule. But he is a fucking prick.
chapterTitle: Chapter IV: The Broken Knight

text: A strike against the King does not go unpunished.
chapterTitle: Chapter V: Fathers and Their Sins

text: In the end... it was just a girl.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: We murdered a lord! We murdered an innocent girl! We slaughtered two hundred knights! The sadistic imbecile you thrust into the council couldn't solve a simple conflict without a massacre!
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: Executing a massacre that will be spoken of for centuries must be terribly exhausting. But I see you're eating and drinking well enough.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: What's your problem, you annoying little bitch?
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: If you ever touch me again, it will be your end. Mother is watching us from the balcony. Smile, and walk away.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: The difference in our marriage is that you chose me, but I was always forced into this!
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: In my seventeen years of life, the only thing you ever gave me was this pin. And you gave it to me for a duty. Not out of love. You look at us as tasks to be managed.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: Experience is everything. Experience triumphs over youth and speed. The men you truly must fear are the experienced ones.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: We are the family chosen by the gods, Vhaemys. We are responsible for all living things in the realm.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: We will wear the blood of this blunder for hundreds of years.
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns

text: Who could possibly love your father?
chapterTitle: Chapter VI: A Crown of Thorns
